Reports by skilled persons: Critical   t hird   p arties  Supervisory   s tatement   | SS 7 /24  November   202 4

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority  Reports by skilled persons: Critical   t hird  p arties  Supervisory statement   |   SS7/24  November   202 4  © Bank of England 202 4  Prudential Regulation Authority | 20 Moorgate | London EC2R 6DA

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   1  Contents  Contents   1  1: Introduction   2  Skilled Persons   2  The use of skilled persons as a supervisory tool   3  Collecting and updating information as a supervisory tool   4  2: PRA and Bank considerations   5  Circumstances relating to a CTP   5  Cost considerations and objectives   5  Considerations relating to PRA and Bank resources   6  3: Appointment of the skilled person   7  Appointment   7  Panel   7  Skills, resources, and conflicts of interest   7  Expectations of skilled persons   8  Scope of report   9  Reporting   9  4: Assisting the skilled person   10  5: Confidential information and privilege   11

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   2  1: Introduction  1.1   This   supervisory statement   (SS)   is addressed to   critical third parties   (CTPs).   A   CTP   is an  entity that has been designated by HM Treasury (HMT) by   regulations   made in exercise of  the power in s312L(1) of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA) as amended  by the   Financial Services and Markets Act 2023   (FSMA 2023).   It is also relevant to   Persons  Connected with a CTP as defined in s ection   312P(10)   of   FSMA given that   s 31P(5)   of   FSMA  states that   ‘ t he power conferred by s166 (reports by skilled person) is exercisable by a  relevant regulator in relation to a   CTP   or a person connected with a   CTP ’ .  1.2   This   SS   set s   out the P rudential   R egulation   A uthority (PRA)   and   the   Bank of England’s  ( the   Bank )   policy on and expectations for the use of the following powers as supervisory tools  as applied by s312P(5) and (6)   of   FSMA:  (i)   s166 (Reports by skilled persons) ;   and  (ii)   s166A   (Appointment of skilled person to collect and update information).  1.3   This statement is intended to be read together with the   Contracts with Skilled Persons  and Delivery of Reports chapter of the Critical   t hird   p arties Part and the Regulatory  Transaction Fees chapter of the Fees Part of the PRA Rulebook and the   Cost of Skilled  Persons Reports and Contracts with Skilled Persons and Delivery of Reports chapters   of the  Bank rules for CTPs. CTPs are also advised to read s312P,   s 166 ,   and   s 166A of FSMA.  Skilled Persons  1.4   Under   s 166 (3)   of   FSMA   (as applied by s312P(5)),   the PRA and/or   the   Bank may appoint  a skilled person to provide it with a report, or require any of the following persons   (‘relevant  persons’)   to provide it with a skilled persons report:  (i)   a   CTP ; or  (ii)   a person connected with a   CTP. 1  1.5   Under s166A (2)   of   FSMA   (as applied by s312P(6)) , the PRA or   the   Bank may require a  CTP to appoint, or may itself appoint, a skilled person to collect or update information.  1   Person connected with a   CTP is defined is defined in s. 312P(1 0 )   of   FSMA and includes both   a member of the  CTP’s group   (as defined in s421 FSMA)   and a controller (s.422   of   FSMA) of the CTP.

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   3  The use of skilled persons as a supervisory tool  1 .6   The appointment of a skilled person to produce a report under   s 166 (3)   of   FSMA is one of  the PRA and   the   Bank’s supervisory tools. The tool may be used   for any matter including :  (i)   for diagnostic purposes: to identify, assess ,   and measure risks;  (ii)   for monitoring purposes: to track the development of identified risks, wherever these  arise;  (iii)   for preventative action: to limit or reduce identified risks and   therefore   prevent them  from crystallising or increasing; or  (iv)   for remedial action: to allow the PRA   and the Bank   to respond to risks when they have  crystallised.  1. 7   The use of the tool could be prompted by   any matter, including :  (i)   a specific requirement by the PRA and/or   the   Bank for information;  (ii)   an analysis of information undertaken by the PRA and/or   the   Bank;  (iii)   an assessment of a situation by the PRA and/or   the   Bank;  (iv)   expert advice or recommendations received by the PRA and/or   the   Bank; or  (v)   a decision by the PRA and/or   the   Bank to seek assurance in relation to a regulatory  return.  1. 8   The tool may also be   used as part of the oversight programme applicable to a CTP . It  may be prompted by   an event or development in relation to a CTP   or   by a need for  verification of information provided to the PRA and/or   the   Bank   or   as part of   ongoing  oversight   of a CTP.  1. 9   The tool is used to support supervisory intervention first and foremost but on occasion the  PRA and/or   the   Bank might choose to obtain a report to support enforcement action being  contemplated.   A   report provided by a skilled person to support supervisory intervention   may  also be   relied on in subsequent enforcement action taken by the PRA and/or   the   Bank.  1. 10   The PRA and   the   Bank encourage CTPs to speak to their   named contact at the PRA or  the   Bank   before commissioning any external review on subject matters which may be of  interest to the regulators. This may avoid   a   CTP from incurring unnecessary costs where the  PRA and/or   the   Bank wishes to make use of skilled persons on the same, or broadly the  same, matter.

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   4  Collecting and updating information as a supervisory tool  1.11   The appointment of a skilled person to collect and update information under section  166A (4)   of   FSMA   is another of the PRA and   the   Bank’s regulatory tools. It may be used  where the PRA and/or   the   Bank considers that a CTP has failed to collect, and keep up - to -  date, information required by the PRA and/or   the   Bank.

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   5  2: PRA and Bank considerations  2.1   When determining whether to use powers under s166 or   s 166A, the PRA and   the   Bank  will consider a range of factors relevant to the   specific case. These factors include those  covered under the headings below.  Circumstances relating to a CTP  2.2   The PRA   and/or the Bank   will consider circumstances relating to a CTP, including, for  example:  (i)   t he   CTP’s relationship with the PRA and/or the Bank :   whether the CTP is being co -  operative;  (ii)   any h istory of similar issues :   whether similar issues have arisen in the past, and if  so, whether timely corrective action was taken;  (iii)   the q uality of a CTP’s systems and records :   whether the PRA   and/or the Bank   has  confidence that the CTP has the ability to provide the required information ;  (iv)   o bjectivity :   whether the PRA   and/or the Bank   has confidence in the CTP’s willingness  and ability to deliver an objective report;  (v)   any c onflicts of interest :   where the subject matter of the report is such that the CTP  and the PRA   and/or the Bank   would benefit from an external third party;   and  (vi)   k nowledge or expertise of the   regulator   or the CTP :   whether the PRA , the   Bank ,   or  the CTP has the appropriate technical expertise or skills .  Cost considerations and objectives  2.3   The PRA and   the   Bank will be mindful of costs, particularly because a CTP   or, where  applicable, the person connected with a CTP   pay s   for the services of the skilled person,  irrespective of whether the skilled person is engaged by a CTP, or contracts directly with the  PRA and/or   the   Bank.  2.4   The PRA and   the   Bank will take into account relevant factors when considering costs,  including, for example:  (i)   whether a CTP may derive some benefit from the work carried out and  recommendations made by the skilled person (eg a better understanding of its

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   6  business and its risk profile, the operation of its information systems, or improvements  to its systems and controls);  (ii)   whether the work to be carried out by the skilled person is work that should otherwise  reasonably have been carried out by the CTP, or by persons instructed by a CTP on  its own initiativ e;  (iii)   whether a CTP’s record keeping and management information systems are poor and:  a.   the required information and documents are not readily available; or  b.   an analysis of the required information cannot readily be performed without  expert assistance;  (iv)   the extent to which there appear to be risks that could threaten the regulators’  objectives;   or  (v)   the perceived probability and seriousness of possible breaches of regulatory  requirements and the possible need for further action.  Considerations relating to PRA   and the Bank   resources  2.5   When deciding whether to use   skilled persons, the PRA and   the   Bank will consider  whether   they have   the necessary expertise   and   available   resources   to conduct the review  themselves , and whether the exercise will be the best use of regulatory resources at that  time.

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   7  3: A ppointment of the skilled person  Appointment  3.1   When the PRA and/or   the   Bank is contracting directly with a skilled person ,   it will itself  appoint the skilled person. Where it is considered appropriate for a CTP to contract with a  skilled person, the regulators may nominate the skilled person. Where   a   CTP is asked to  propose a skilled person, the PRA and/or   the   Bank will direct the CTP to the skilled persons  in the relevant lot(s) on the Panel 2   and ask the CTP to submit a shortlist of nominees for the  PRA and/or   the   Bank to approve. Where   a   CTP is contracting with a skilled person, it  remains   the   CTP’s responsibility to assess the individual appropriateness of the skilled  person   irrespective that the skilled person is in the relevant lot on the Panel .  3.2 Where a person connected to a CTP appoints a skilled person ,   the PRA and   the   Bank  expect that they would include similar contractual terms to those set out in the   C ontracts with  Skilled Persons and Delivery of Reports Part s   of   the PRA and Bank   R ulebooks.  Panel  3. 3   To enable the PRA and/or   the   Bank to use its power to appoint a skilled person ,   a panel  of skilled persons (‘the Panel’) has been developed for use.  3. 4   Where the PRA and/or   the   Bank is contracting directly with the skilled person, they will  conduct a tender process, where appropriate, to identify the most suitable skilled person.  This will include the considerations detailed below.  Skills, resources ,   and conflicts of interest  3. 5   When considering whether to nominate, approve ,   or appoint a skilled person, the PRA  and/or   the   Bank will consider the circumstances of the case, including whether the proposed  skilled person appears to have:  (i)   the necessary skills and any relevant specialised knowledge   relating to the  systemic third party   services provided by the CTP and the particular subject matter  area;  (ii)   sufficient resources   to complete the report or collect or update the information within  the time expected by the PRA and/or   the   Bank;   and  2   The skilled person Panel is a joint FCA and Bank framework of pre - approved   suppliers that the Bank can  choose to buy from over a set period. It is divided into several distinct lots which break down the types of  services into specific groups. Each lot represents a subject area of expertise for skilled person services.

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   8  (iii)   any professional difficulty, potential conflict of interest, or insufficient  detachment   –   such   matters would include:  a.   matters already reported on by the skilled person ;  b.   matters that are likely to be contentious and may result in disciplinary or  other enforcement action against a CTP, its management, shareholders ,   or  controllers; or  c.   matters that the skilled person has been involved in, in another   capacity.  Expectations of skilled persons  3. 6   The PRA and   the   Bank expect a skilled person to clarify or discuss any matters that are  raised by either regulator regarding a contract before it is finalised.  3. 7   The PRA and   the Bank   expect the skilled person normally to give a periodic update on  progress and issues during the engagement, the frequency of which is to be discussed with  either regulator. This should allow for a re - focusing of the report if necessary.  3. 8   The channel of communication would normally be directly between the skilled person and  the PRA and/or   the   Bank. The skilled person would usually be expected to keep the CTP  informed of any communication between the skilled person and either regulator. It may be  appropriate for the skilled person to communicate matters of material significance to either  the PRA and/or   the   Bank set out in   chapter 12 (1)(b)   of the CTP Part   of the PRA and Bank  R ulebooks , without first informing the CTP.  3. 9   If the PRA and/or   the   Bank is considering asking for additional information during the  engagement, it will take into consideration the cost of the skilled person complying with the  request and the benefit that the regulators may derive from the information. In most cases,  the PRA and   the   Bank will not need to request a skilled person to give it source data,  documents ,   and working papers. However, the PRA   and/or the Bank   may do so when it  reasonably believes that this information will be relevant to any investigation it may be  conducting, or any action it may consider taking against the CTP.  3. 10   I n the relatively rare situation where enforcement action is anticipated as a likely  outcome ,   the PRA and/or   the   Bank would normally signal this to the skilled person prior to  work commencing, as this will impact the type of evidence which should be provided in  support of the findings in the skilled person report.

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   9  Scope of report  3.1 1   The PRA and   the   Bank will normally contact the relevant persons to discuss the  regulatory requirements before finalising its notice to require a report or the updating or  collection of information by a skilled person.  3.1 2   The PRA and   the   Bank will give written notification to the relevant persons of the  purpose of the report or collection or updating of information, its scope, the timetable for  completion and any other relevant matters. The PRA and   the   Bank will state the matters  which the report is to contain, or the information ,   which is to be collected or updated, as well  as any requirements to the report’s format.  3.1 3   The PRA and   the   Bank attach importance to   having   a timetable for each report and to  the skilled person, with the co - operation of the relevant persons ,   keeping to that timetable.  Reporting  3.1 4   Where the skilled person is appointed by a CTP, the PRA and/or   the   Bank will normally  require the skilled person to report to the regulator through   the   CTP. Where the skilled  person is to be appointed by the PRA and/or   the   Bank itself, the skilled person will report  directly to the regulator(s).   Regardless of how the skilled person is appointed, they will  usually be in regular contact with the PRA   and/or the Bank   throughout the process.  3.1 5   The skilled person is expected to provide the PRA and/or   the   Bank with identical copies  of any draft report or relevant information at the same time they are issued to the CTP. The  PRA and   the   Bank expects that   the   CTP will be given the opportunity to provide written  comments on the report or the collection of the relevant information prior to its final  submission to the regulator.  3.1 6   The PRA and   the   Bank will normally specify a time limit within which it expects the  skilled person to deliver the report or collect or update the relevant information.  3.1 7   If the skilled person becomes aware that the report, or collection or updating of the  relevant information may not be delivered on time, they should inform the PRA   and/or the  Bank   and the CTP as soon as possible. Where the skilled person is appointed by a CTP and  becomes aware that there may be difficulties delivering the report or collecting or updating  the relevant information within cost estimates, the skilled person should advise the CTP .  3.1 8   The PRA and/or   the   Bank may meet with the CTP and the skilled person together to  discuss the final report. The PRA and /or   the   Bank may also wish to discuss the final report  with the skilled person but without the CTP .

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   10  4:   Assisting the skilled person  4.1   A CTP is expected to provide reasonable assistance, which should include:  (i)   access at all reasonable business hours for the skilled person to a CTP’s records that  relate to the provision of a material service in whatever form;  (ii)   the provision of such information and explanations as the skilled person reasonably  considers necessary or desirable for the performance of   their   duties; and  (iii)   permitting a skilled person to obtain such information directly from a CTP’s auditor as  they reasonably consider necessary or desirable for the proper performance of   their  d uties.

Bank of England   |   Prudential Regulation Authority   Page   11  5:   Confidential information and privilege  5.1   Within the legal constraints that apply, the PRA and/or   the   Bank may pass on to a skilled  person any information   that   they consider relevant to the skilled person’s function. A skilled  person, being a primary recipient under   s 348   of   FSMA (Restrictions on disclosure of  confidential information by   the   PRA), is bound by the confidentiality provisions in Part XXIII   of  FSMA (Public record, disclosure of information and   co - operation) as regards confidential  information the skilled person receives from the PRA /Bank   or directly from a CTP or other  persons.  5.2   A skilled person may not pass on confidential information without lawful authority . Lawful  authority may exist   for example, where an exception applies under FSMA (Disclosure of  Confidential Information) Regulations 2001 (SI 2001/2188) or with the consent of the person  from whom that information was received and (if different) the person to whom the  information relates. The PRA   and/or   the   Bank   will indicate to a skilled person if there is any  matter which cannot be discussed with the CTP.
